title: Facilities, Racks, Cabling, Power, and Environment · Protocol Recall
summary: Plan an MDF, IDFs, racks, patching, power, cooling, security, and maintenance access for a new site. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a facilities-ready network installation package after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Place MDF and IDF spaces, rack units, pathways, grounding, clearances, and service loops from building constraints.
Document patch panels, fiber distribution panels, labels, polarity, ports, pathways, and change ownership.
Align port-side intake or exhaust, hot and cold airflow, lockable access, and maintenance reach.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.instruction:

content.text: An IDF is simply a smaller rack and needs no distance or power planning. This fails the Net Mdf Idf Racks contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

stimulus.title: Net Environment Fire case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: network physical installation. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Any fire extinguisher is safe for energized network equipment.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls. Artifact under review: a complete first version of a facilities-ready network installation package while every earlier competency remains active.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Net Environment Fire, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Environment Fire applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Monitor temperature and humidity and select compatible detection, suppression, water, dust, and contamination controls.
